name: Jaraíz de la Vera from Garganta la Olla — circular of the Mojón Blanco
name_original: Jaraíz de la Vera desde Garganta la Olla — circular del Mojón Blanco
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Garganta La Olla traverse a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, including mountain passes and rolling hills. The region features a mix of open countryside and forested areas, providing varied terrain for road cyclists. Routes often include challenging ascents and descents, with views of the surrounding valleys and peaks. This area offers a diverse environment for road cycling, from demanding climbs to more moderate paths. Best road cycling routes around Garganta La Olla The most popular road cycling route is Jaraiz de la Vera from the Piscina Natural El Lago — circular by Barrado and Garganta La Olla, a 43.0 miles (69.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain, offering a challenging ride through varied scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Jaraíz de la Vera from Garganta la Olla — circular of the Mojón Blanco, a difficult 25.9 miles (41.6 km) path. This route navigates through hilly terrain with notable climbs, providing panoramic views of the area. Local road cyclists also love the Piornal Pass – Puerto de Piornal (1,285 m) loop from Jaraíz de la Vera, a 54.4 miles (87.6 km) trail leading through mountain passes, often completed in about 4 hours 57 minutes. Road cycling around Garganta La Olla is defined by mountain passes, significant elevation changes, and scenic valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Garganta La Olla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 9 reviews. More than 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Garganta La Olla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Garganta La Olla?
A: There are over 75 road cycling routes around Garganta La Olla, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Garganta La Olla?
A: While many routes in Garganta La Olla feature significant elevation changes and challenging climbs, there are a few easier options. The region offers 2 routes classified as easy, and 40 as moderate, providing choices for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Garganta La Olla?
A: Road cycling routes in Garganta La Olla are characterized by significant elevation changes, including mountain passes and rolling hills. You'll encounter a mix of open countryside and forested areas, with challenging ascents and descents that often provide panoramic views of the surrounding valleys and peaks.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Garganta La Olla is well-suited for experienced riders seeking a challenge. There are 35 routes classified as difficult, featuring substantial elevation gains and demanding climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piornal Pass – Puerto de Piornal (1,285 m) loop from Jaraíz de la Vera&lt;/a&gt; covers 54.4 miles (87.6 km) with nearly 2,000 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find loop road cycling routes in the area?
A: Many road cycling routes around Garganta La Olla are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292369713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaraiz de la Vera from the Piscina Natural El Lago — circular by Barrado and Garganta La Olla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292317406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaraíz de la Vera from Garganta la Olla — circular of the Mojón Blanco&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: The region boasts several natural attractions that can be explored. You might encounter waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/874276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Trabuquete&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, or natural monuments such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte River&lt;/a&gt; also flows through the area.

Q: Are there viewpoints accessible from the road cycling trails?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of Garganta La Olla offers several scenic viewpoints. One notable spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrero de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing expansive vistas of the landscape.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Garganta La Olla?
A: Given the region's mountain passes and significant elevation changes, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher altitudes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Garganta La Olla?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 9 reviews. Road cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the diverse scenery ranging from open countryside to forested areas, and the panoramic views offered by the mountain passes.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, Garganta La Olla offers 40 routes classified as moderate. These routes provide a good balance of challenge and accessibility, often featuring notable climbs without the extreme demands of the most difficult trails. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuacos de Yuste – Lake Alonso Vega loop from Jaraíz de la Vera&lt;/a&gt;, a 43.3-mile (69.7 km) route with over 700 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are the typical distances for road cycling routes in this region?
A: The distances for road cycling routes in Garganta La Olla vary significantly. You can find routes ranging from shorter, moderate rides to longer, more challenging excursions. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292317406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaraíz de la Vera from Garganta la Olla — circular of the Mojón Blanco&lt;/a&gt; is 25.9 miles (41.6 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Piornal (1,285 m) – Garganta la Olla loop from Piornal&lt;/a&gt; covers 30.9 miles (49.7 km).
tips: A beautiful mountain town of Garganta la Ollа in the foothills of the Sierra de Tormantos, dominated by the Sierra de Gredos at 1400 meters above sea level.

The ascent from the town of Garganta la Olla is tough. Long 13.36 km with an average slope of 5.2%, you gain almost 700 m to reach the pass at 1,285 meters above sea level before descending towards Piornal. Beautiful views of the mountain peaks and valleys of Jerte.
Waterfall with a lot of water in spring
